demographics.living_situation: lives at home, usually independent; adult child visiting and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel incredibly dizzy every time I stand up, my stomach is constantly turning, and I simply cannot keep track of all these pills.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The dizziness and nausea have been building for about a week and she had a little fall yesterday — her adult child is worried and rang after hours.

symptom_narrative.onset: the dizziness and nausea have crept up over the past week or so
symptom_narrative.duration: about a week, and a fall yesterday
symptom_narrative.character: she goes very dizzy and unsteady the moment she stands up, and her stomach is turning most of the day
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that she's frightened to stand up on her own now
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: dizziness in her head when she rises; queasy in her stomach
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the dizziness comes on every time she stands; the nausea is there most of the day
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting or lying back down helps the dizziness a bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up quickly makes it much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she had a minor fall yesterday; the child mentions she had some nosebleeds recently and was a bit muddled one day last week
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the child says she's had side-effect problems from her tablets before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually independent, but right now she's barely moving around because standing makes her dizzy and she's scared of falling

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: high blood pressure, on tablets for it
past_medical_history.how_managed: several blood-pressure tablets from different doctors

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a joint condition she sees a rheumatologist for
some recent nosebleeds and one muddled/confused spell last week

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: about twelve different tablets in total, from a heart doctor, a joint doctor and the GP
a heart tablet that slows the pulse
a strong painkiller for her back
an anti-sickness tablet that was added after the painkiller upset her stomach

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and usually manages independently; the medication routine is confusing — some pills in the morning, some at noon, some at night — and the adult child is helping sort them out tonight

objective_data_offered.value: her home blood-pressure monitor read about 140 over 85 sitting, then about 95 over 60 when she stood up
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer read the numbers off a home monitor; device not validated and technique not observed

objective_data_offered.value: frail, anxious-looking older woman sitting very carefully in a chair; a dining table covered in loose pill bottles, mismatched blister packs and handwritten medication notes is visible behind her

adult child says she gets very dizzy and nearly goes over every time she stands, had a minor fall yesterday, has been off her food and nauseated all week, had a couple of nosebleeds recently, and had one confused/muddled spell last week
adult child reports she keeps mixing up which tablets to take and when, and thinks she may sometimes double up or miss doses
